text: Chapter 1. RowPack stores one logical record at a time, but long text can be split into smaller continuation rows for training. This sample is intentionally small so the converter command in the README works in a fresh checkout.

Camera frames, robot state, calibration notes, and plain text can live together in RowPack. For very large corpora, use the searchable document index so a book title points back to the range of rows that came from the original document.

This row has enough repeated prose to demonstrate splitting. A converter can keep the first chunk with metadata, then write continuation chunks that only carry the split text field and _rowpack_split metadata. Training code can consume the chunks independently or reconstruct the source document later. This row has enough repeated prose to demonstrate splitting.
